LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: AT THE START OF THE HEARING, THE COURT READ ITS TENTATIVE RULING, WHICH NEITHER PARTY OPPOSED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS YONGYIN LU AND AITING WU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFENDANTS' DEFAULT ENTERED ON 9-28-12 AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED 11-20-12 IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO RE-FILING THE MOTION PURSUANT TO CCP 473.5 OR CCP 473(B). DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT CCP 473(B) FACTORS HAVE BEEN MET. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS YONGYIN LU, AITING WU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 473.5. EVEN IF THE COURT ASSUMES THAT THE SERVICE BY PUBLICATION WAS PROPER, DEFENDANTS ESTABLISH THAT THEY DID NOT HAVE NOTICE OF THE ACTION IN TIME TO DEFEND THE ACTION. DEFENDANTS HAVE LEAVE TO FILE THEIR PROPOSED ANSWER. THE REQUEST FOR FEES AND OTHER CONDITIONS IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

MICHAEL RAIFSNIDER, ESQ., 415.398.9001, FOR DEFENDANT YONG YIN LU.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS YONGYIN LU, AITING WU'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is granted pursuant to CCP 473.5.
